title: ranunculus sceleratus var. multifidus
intro: Ranunculus sceleratus var. multifidus, also known as multifid buttercup, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family and is native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is often employed to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and urinary tract infections. The multifid buttercup is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce swelling, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory and urinary issues. Traditionally, the plant can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is sometimes used in herbal remedies to treat a range of health issues.
